hypothesis: The prepared Q0.24 top-k score comparator remains the smallest useful no-board gate because it validates the full-vocab streamed top-k score rule without DDR3 integration or synthesis. This invocation should promote it only if Verilator or Python execution recovers; otherwise it should preserve the prepared RTL, record the exact process-startup blocker, and leave the next invocation with a concrete simulator command.

commands.cmd: sed reads of h2-int8-l2-selftest-board-comparison.json, h2-v4k-scale-up-summary.json, and h2-vocab-memory-surface-score.json

commands.cmd: apply_patch add/update of invocation 25 blocked artifacts, run handoff, and AUTONIGHT_STATUS.md

metrics.hardware_block_reason: no board access in this run

decision.rationale: The prepared comparator RTL and testbench still align with the Q0.24 expected-state surface by static readback, but Verilator, Python, git, listing commands, and even a trivial true command fail before tool startup with the sandbox loopback error. This invocation therefore cannot produce simulator PASS evidence, Python replay evidence, synthesis evidence, or a board-facing promotion.
decision.next_gate: Recover command execution, then run: verilator --binary --timing -Wall rtl/task6/task6_q024_topk_score_compare.sv rtl/task6/task6_q024_topk_score_compare_tb.sv && ./obj_dir/Vtask6_q024_topk_score_compare_tb. If it passes, emit h2-q024-topk-comparator-cutout-result.json. If it fails, fix only the tiny comparator/testbench before moving to full-vocab rowwise top-k replay or attention scorecards.
